source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
THE MISSION

Nolla is building AI doctors so that top-quality healthcare is accessible to everyone. Nolla Skin is the #1 medical skincare treatment app in the U.S. App Store. We've treated thousands of acne patients in the U.S., scanned 1% of Norway's population for skin cancer, and our in-house clinical models are state of the art on clinical benchmarks. We just launched NollaMD, our urgent care app, and we're building dedicated specialty apps for conditions like birth control and allergies. We raised a $4.5M seed from General Catalyst.



THE ROLE

We're hiring our first dedicated product designer. You'll own design across everything we ship and define what Nolla looks and feels like as a product company. You'll work directly with the founding team and your work reaches patients immediately.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Own end-to-end design for Nolla Skin plus the new specialty apps we're launching

 - Translate dense clinical and scientific information into experiences people actually want to engage with

 - Make complex healthcare feel obvious

 - Build a design system that ties our growing portfolio of apps together. Cohesive, but flexible enough for each app to have its own personality

 - Go from a metric, user problem, or business goal → product concept → user flow → polished design

 - Partner closely with engineering to ship fast and iterate based on real user data
   
   

WHAT YOU BRING

 - 4+ years designing consumer apps, ideally including mobile

 - A portfolio that shows the work is polished: interactions feel considered, type and layout are tight, the apps feel magical to use

 - You think in flows and systems, not just screens. You can scope and design a feature end-to-end

 - Strong opinions about how to make information-dense products legible and beautiful

 - Fluent in Figma; comfortable with prototyping and at least light motion

 - You're passionate about healthcare even if you haven't worked in it. You've worked at small companies before and ship fast

BONUS POINTS

 - Experience designing across a multi-product ecosystem

 - Opinions about why most consumer healthcare UX is bad
   
   

ROLE LOGISTICS, COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Role Type: Product / Design

 - Salary: $160,000-$220,000, based on experience

 - Job Type: Full-time

 - Work Setup: In-person, New York City

 - Equity: Meaningful equity, commensurate with experience

 - Health Insurance: Medical, dental, and vision

 - HSA/FSA: Eligible

 - Time Off: Flexible, unlimited vacation

 - Additional Perks: Meal stipends, team retreats

 - Work Hours: Flexible but demanding. We're building something that matters

 - Growth: Founding team members step into expanded roles as we scale

source_jobs.title: Founding AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE MISSION

Nolla is building AI doctors so that top-quality healthcare is accessible to everyone. Nolla Skin is the #1 medical skincare treatment app in the U.S. App Store. We've treated thousands of acne patients in the U.S., scanned 1% of Norway's population for skin cancer, and our in-house clinical models are state of the art on clinical benchmarks. We just launched NollaMD, our urgent care app, and we're building dedicated specialty apps for conditions like birth control and allergies. We raised a $4.5M seed from General Catalyst.



THE ROLE

This is an AI engineering role, not an ML engineering role. You won't be training models (unless you want to). You will wire LLMs and other custom models into production systems where the cost of getting it wrong is high, build the eval infrastructure that tells us when we're getting it right, and ship the backend surface area that keeps the product moving. You'll work directly with the founding team and your work reaches patients immediately.



WHAT YOU'LL BUILD

App Backends

 - Build and maintain backends for our existing apps, plus stand up new ones as we launch new specialty apps

 - Own backend systems end to end, from schema design to production

Clinician Tooling

 - Build the clinician-facing dashboards and tooling our medical team uses to review AI output

 - Develop internal tools that clinical ops and physicians use daily

AI Systems

 - Work on prompts, tool calling, and inference optimization for the AI features in our apps

 - Build evals and observability that give us confidence in what we deploy. You'll define what "good" looks like for our AI systems

 - Implement abstention, routing, and fallback strategies that favor safety and correctness

Jack-of-all-Trades Surface Area

 - Support a wide range of work: payments, user flows, clinical infra, model deployment

 - Help architect systems that support rapid product expansion: new verticals, new states, new clinical modalities
   
   

WHAT YOU BRING

 - 4+ years of backend, platform, or product engineering experience

 - You ship your own things. You have a portfolio: side projects, open source, apps you launched

 - You've worked on small teams and built things from zero

 - Comfortable with the AI engineering toolkit: prompts, evals, tool calling, RAG, common LLM APIs. You have opinions on when each tool is the right one

 - Strong product instincts: you don't need a PM to tell you what to build, you can scope and ship

 - Strong TypeScript and Python

BONUS POINTS

 - Regulated industry experience (healthcare, fintech)

 - Comfortable with infra and can deploy what you build (Docker, CI/CD, etc.)

 - Familiarity with FHIR, HL7, or healthcare data standards

 - Experience with observability stacks: Langfuse, Sentry, or similar

 - Prior work in clinical AI, digital health, or other regulated domains
   
   

STACK

TypeScript · Python · Node.js · PostgreSQL · AWS · OpenAI / Anthropic APIs · EMR systems · Eval frameworks



ROLE LOGISTICS, COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Role Type: Engineering

 - Salary: $160,000-$220,000, based on experience

 - Job Type: Full-time

 - Work Setup: In-person, New York City

 - Equity: Meaningful equity, commensurate with experience

 - Health Insurance: Medical, dental, and vision

 - HSA/FSA: Eligible

 - Time Off: Flexible, unlimited vacation

 - Additional Perks: Meal stipends, team retreats

 - Work Hours: Flexible but demanding. We're building something that matters

 - Growth: Founding team members step into expanded roles as we scale

source_jobs.title: Social Media Coordinator (Part-time)
source_jobs.description:
THE MISSION

Nolla is building AI doctors so that top-quality healthcare is accessible to everyone. Nolla Skin is the #1 medical skincare treatment app in the U.S. App Store. We've treated thousands of acne patients in the U.S., scanned 1% of Norway's population for skin cancer, and our in-house clinical models are state of the art on clinical benchmarks. We just launched NollaMD, our urgent care app, and we're building dedicated specialty apps for conditions like birth control and allergies. We raised a $4.5M seed from General Catalyst.


THE ROLE

We're hiring our first dedicated social media coordinator. You'll keep Nolla's feeds active and cohesive across Instagram, TikTok, Twitter, and LinkedIn, and own the day-to-day rhythm that keeps content moving. You'll work directly with the COO and marketing team — flagging gaps, keeping the posting schedule on track, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks.

This is a great fit for someone currently enrolled in a bachelor's program or up to two years out of college who has genuine taste and wants to grow inside a fast-moving consumer health startup. As Nolla scales, there's a clear path to a broader role spanning creative direction, content production, video editing, and design.


WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Manage posting across Instagram, TikTok, Twitter, and LinkedIn via Buffer. Instagram and TikTok run daily; Twitter and LinkedIn twice a week.

 - Choose which creatives to post and in what sequence so each feed looks considered and tells a consistent story.

 - Write captions that sound like a person — sharp, on-brand, no filler.

 - Proactively flag when content is missing, deadlines are at risk, or the calendar has gaps — keep the team accountable without being asked.

 - Respond to comments and DMs, and engage with top posts in skin care and wellness.

 - Handle community management: flag standout user content, reach out to offer free months to posts that go viral, and nurture the Nolla community.


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Genuine taste in skin care and wellness content, and a clear sense of how good brands show up on Instagram and TikTok.

 - Strong organizational instincts — you track what's due, spot what's missing, and follow up before things slip.

 - Experience managing posting across multiple accounts and building feeds that look considered.

 - Strong caption writing — you write things people actually read, and you have opinions about what should go up.

 - Fluent in Buffer or a comparable scheduling tool.

 - Passion for health, wellness, and consumer products.


BONUS POINTS

 - Your own social presence with an engaged following in health, wellness, or beauty.

 - Experience with content calendars, Notion, or similar planning tools.

 - Opinions about why most healthcare brands are boring on social — and concrete ideas for how to do it differently.


ROLE LOGISTICS, COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

Part-time contract, roughly 10 hours per week. $15–20/hr. Fully remote. You'll work directly with the COO and marketing team, with a clear path to a broader role spanning creative direction, content production, video editing, and design as Nolla scales.

notes: AI healthcare startup with a strong product focus
